rulings.Case Title: PETER CHILDRESS VS. WILLIAM MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF PETER CHILDRESS'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint is granted. The new claims are based on facts that have previously been alleged. No prejudice is shown by defendant. The motion is timely. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GRANT & WEBER, A CORPORATION VS. MAX FRUMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT MAX FRUMES'S Motion To Vacate Judgment is denied. Defendant fails to demonstrate that he was not properly served with the summons and complaint as declared under penalty of perjury by the registered process server. The mailed summons and complaint and the request for entry of default were not returned to plaintiff's counsel. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY TAM VS. DOMINIC TSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents; Order That Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Order For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 2. DEFENDANT DOMINIC TSE'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents; Order That Matters Specified In Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Order For Sanctions. Granted, no opposition filed, if proof of service presented at the hearing. Defendant is awarded $1,402.50 in sanctions. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J0HN CARLTON VS. SUSAN SALISBURY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Vacating Amended Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT RANDALL A. BLACKBURN'S Motion For An Order Vacating Amended Default is granted. Defendant makes a sufficient showing of excusable neglect and the law favors adjudicating cases on their merits. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT CHARLES LOUCKS' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue is denied. Venue selection clauses are unenforceable. See Alexander v. Superior Court (2003) 114 Cal. App. 4th 723. None of the defendants in this case reside in Los Angeles. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MICHAEL COOMBS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 19, 2010, line 15, PETITIONERS MICHAEL COOMS, TAMARA WOODS' Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration And For Appointment Of Neutral Arbitrator is granted. Mediation failed to resolve this dispute. Respondent signed the tenancy in common agreement which contains a clear and unambiguous arbitration agreement. Respondent fails to show that the arbitration agreement is invalid or unenforceable. Respondent has not objected to the proposed arbitrator. The court appoints the Hon. V. Gene McDonald (Ret.) of JAMS as the arbitrator in this matter. Respondent to pay reasonable attorney fees and costs to petitioners in the amount of $2,610 no later than 60 days from notice of this order pursuant to Section 14.3 G of the agreement. =(302/CWW)
